subject: science
question: Name the process of converting vegetable oil to vegetable ghee.
answer: Hydrogenation

subject: science
question: List two secondary sexual characters of males that are different from that of females.
answer: Moustaches, Beard, Hoarse voice, Thick growth of hair on the body (Any two)

subject: science
question: Write the main function of Ozone in the upper atmosphere.
answer: To prevent UV radiation from reaching the earth.

subject: science
question: When a concave mirror is placed facing the Sun, the Sun’s rays converge to a point 10 cm from the mirror. Now, if you place a 2 cm long candles flame 20 cm away on the principal axis of the mirror, where would you place a screen to obtain the image of the candle? What would see the size of the image? Draw a ray diagram to justify your answer.
answer: f = -10 cm (since the image is formed at the focus). Now u = -20 cm, i.e. the candle flame is at C. The image would form at C and would be of the same size. Ray diagram: Object at C (2f), image forms at C, real, inverted, same size.

subject: science
question: If you find coliform bacteria in a sample of water collected from a river, what would you conclude? Give reason for such kind of water pollution.
answer: Conclusion: Water is polluted by sewage. Coliform is a group of bacteria found in human intestine whose presence in river water indicates contamination by disease causing micro-organisms.

subject: science
question: Which natural resources are called the “biodiversity hot spots”? How these hot spots maintain ecological balance?
answer: Forests are the biodiversity hot spots. Range of different life forms plays an important role in maintaining ecological balance as they all depend on each other for their survival.

subject: science
question: What are isomers? “Isomers of the first three members of alkane series are not possible”. Give reason to justify this statement and draw structures of two isomers of butane, C₄H₁₀.
answer: Isomers: Compounds which have same molecular formula and different structural formula. In first three members of alkane series, branching is not possible, therefore isomers are not possible. Two isomers of butane C4H10: n-butane (CH3-CH2-CH2-CH3) and isobutane (CH3-CH(CH3)-CH3).

subject: science
question: An organic compound ‘X’ is a liquid at room temperature. It is also a very good solvent and has the molecular formula C₂H₆O. On oxidation ‘X’ gives ‘Y’ which gives brisk effervescence on reacting with NaHCO₃. X reacts with Y in the presence of conc. H₂SO₄ to give another compound ‘Z’ which has a pleasant smell. Identify X, Y and Z. Also write chemical equations to show the formation of Y and Z.
answer: X = Ethanol (C2H5OH), Y = Ethanoic acid (CH3COOH), Z = Ester (CH3COOC2H5). Equation for Y: C2H5OH + [O] → CH3COOH (oxidation). Equation for Z: C2H5OH + CH3COOH → CH3COOC2H5 + H2O (esterification with conc. H2SO4).

subject: science
question: An element ‘A’ belongs to 3rd period and 17th group of the periodic table. a) Write atomic number and electronic configuration of ‘A’. b) State whether A is a metal or a non-metal. c) Write the nature of bond formed if A reacts with another element B of electronic configuration 2, 8, 1. d) Write the chemical formula of the compound formed.
answer: a) Atomic number of A = 17; Electronic configuration = 2, 8, 7. b) A is a non-metal. c) B has electronic configuration 2,8,1 — B will give one electron to A to form an ionic compound — Ionic bond. d) Formula of the compound: BA (i.e., B+A⁻, for example KCl if B is K).

subject: science
question: Consider the following elements: ₄Be, ₉F, ₁₉K, ₂₀Ca. a) Select the element having one electron in the outermost shell. b) Select two elements of the same group. c) Write the formula and state the nature of the compound formed when the element K reacts with an element X of electronic configuration 2, 8, 7.
answer: a) K (electronic configuration 2,8,8,1). b) Be and Ca — Be(4) = 2,2; Ca(20) = 2,8,8,2 (both Group 2). c) K+X⁻ → KX; K = 2,8,8,1 and X = 2,8,7 — ionic compound formed.

subject: science
question: Explain with the help of labelled diagrams the development that takes place in each of the body parts of a Planaria where body is cut into three pieces. Name this process and give an example of an organism in which a similar process can be observed.
answer: This process is called regeneration. When a Planaria is cut into three pieces, each of its pieces grow into separate individual; specialized cells proliferate; mass of cells get differentiated into various cells and tissues. Another example of an organism showing the characteristic is Hydra.

subject: science
question: List any four methods of contraception used by humans. How does the use of these methods have a direct effect on the health and prosperity of a family?
answer: Four methods: (i) Mechanical/Barrier method (ii) Use of hormonal preparations (iii) Use of loop/Copper-T/IUCD (iv) Surgical method (Tubectomy/Vasectomy). Effect on health and prosperity (Any two): Health of women is maintained; Better attention to children by the parents; More resources for better living standard.

subject: science
question: List two functions each of the following parts of human female reproductive system: a) Ovaries b) Fallopian tubes c) Uterus.
answer: Ovaries: (i) Production of female sex hormone. (ii) Production of female gamete. Fallopian tube: (i) Transfer of female gamete from ovary. (ii) Site of fertilization. Uterus: (i) Implantation of zygote. (ii) Nourishment of developing embryo.

subject: science
question: “Different species use different strategies to determine sex of a new born individual. It can be environmental cues or genetically determined”. Justify this statement giving reason/example.
answer: Environmental Cue: In some animals, the temperature at which fertilized eggs are kept determines whether the developing animal in egg is male or female; in some animals like snail, individual can change sex. Genetical Cue: A child who inherits an X chromosome from her father will be a girl and one who inherits a Y chromosome from the father will be a boy.

subject: science
question: List in tabular form, two distinguishing features between the acquired traits and the inherited traits with one example of each.
answer: Acquired Traits: Does not bring about change in the DNA of germ cells; Cannot be passed on to the progeny; Cannot direct evolution; Example: Acquiring knowledge / loss of body parts. Inherited Traits: Brings about change in the DNA of the germ cells; Can be passed on to the progeny; Can direct evolution; Example: Skin colour / colour of the eyes.

subject: science
question: A student has to project a three times magnified image of a candle flame on a wall. Name the type of the lens (converging/diverging) required for the purpose. If the candle flame is at a distance of 6 m from the wall, find the focal length of the lens.
answer: Converging lens (Convex lens). u + v = 6 m, v = 3u, so u = 1.5 m, v = 4.5 m. f = uv/(u+v) = (1.5 × 4.5)/(1.5 + 4.5) = 1.125 m.

subject: science
question: A child while playing with his father’s spectacles burnt a hole in a piece of tissue paper by focusing the image of the Sun on it. a) Name the defect of vision his father is suffering from. b) List two causes of the defect. c) Draw a ray diagram to show how this defect may be corrected using a suitable lens.
answer: a) Hypermetropia. b) Two causes: (i) The focal length of the eye lens is too long. (ii) The eye ball has become too small. c) Ray diagram showing correction using a convex (converging) lens.
chapter: The Human Eye and the Colourful World

subject: science
question: Some residents of your colony are planning to cut trees to create a place for parking vehicles. You are upset by knowing all this and wanted to do something to create awareness about the importance of trees. (a) List any three reasons that you will use to convince residents of your colony not to cut the existing trees. (b) List any three values that would be inculcated amongst them with such approach.
answer: (a) Any three: importance of green plants in reducing air pollution; reducing noise levels; aesthetic value; sapling distribution; giving potted plants in place of bouquet; creating awareness. (b) Any three: To protect and improve the natural environment; social awareness; environmental conservation; eco-friendly approach; value for life.

subject: science
question: (a) State two main reasons for carbon forming a very large number of compounds. (b) Give reason why carbon forms compounds (i) Namely by Covalent bonding (ii) Having low melting and boiling points. (c) What happens when (Write chemical equations of the reactions): (i) A piece of sodium metal is added to ethanol. (ii) Ethanoic acid reacts with sodium hydroxide.
answer: (a) (i) Catenation: Unique ability of carbon to form bonds with other atoms of carbon giving rise to long chains. (ii) Tetravalency: Carbon has a valency of 4, capable of bonding with four other atoms. (b) (i) Carbon has 4 electrons in outermost shell — losing or gaining 4 electrons is not possible for energy considerations, hence shares electrons to form covalent bonds. (ii) Forces of attraction between molecules are not very strong, hence low melting and boiling points. (c) (i) 2Na + 2C2H5OH → 2C2H5ONa + H2. (ii) NaOH + CH3COOH → CH3COONa + H2O.

subject: science
question: A. Draw a longitudinal section of a flower and label the following parts: a) Part that develops into a fruit b) Part that produces pollen grain c) Part that transfers male gametes d) Part that is sticky to trap pollen. B. Write the names of the parts labeled as A, B, C, D in the diagram given below (showing pollen tube, ovary, female gamete and pollen grain).
answer: A. Labelled diagram required: a) Ovary b) Anther c) Pollen tube d) Stigma. B. A – Pollen grain; B – Pollen tube; C – Ovary; D – Female gamete.

subject: science
question: A. How does speciation take place? B. Define the term GENE. C. The gene for red hair is recessive to the gene for black hair. What will be the hair colour of a child if he inherits a gene for red colour from his mother and a gene for black hair from his father? Express with the help of flow chart.
answer: A. Speciation may take place by (i) Migration (ii) Natural selection (iii) Mutation (iv) Genetic Drift (Any two). B. Gene: Segment of DNA which is functional and are made of nucleic acids and protein. C. Red hair – Mother – Recessive (bb); Black hair – Father – Dominant (BB). Cross: BB × bb → F1 = Bb (Black hair). The child will have black hair.

subject: science
question: A. Explain the formation of rainbow in the sky with the help of a diagram. List the three phenomena of light involved. Which colour – violet or red appears at top of the rainbow? B. What is the difference in colours of the sun observed during sunrise/sunset and noon? Give explanation for each.
answer: A. Water droplets act like small prisms. They refract and disperse incident sunlight, then reflect it internally and finally refract it again when it comes out. Three phenomena: refraction, dispersion, internal reflection. Red appears at the top of the rainbow. B. At sunrise/sunset, sunlight travels a larger distance through atmosphere, more blue light scattered, so sun appears orange/red. At noon, distance is less, less scattering, sun looks white.
chapter: The Human Eye and the Colourful World

subject: science
question: (i) Which property of concave mirror is utilized for using them as shaving mirrors? (ii) Light passes through a rectangular glass slab and through a triangular glass prism. Using proper ray diagram, explain in what way does the direction of the two emergent beams differs with respect to the incident beam of light. (iii) A concave lens has a focal length of 50 cm. Calculate its power.
answer: (i) When an object is placed between the pole and focus of concave mirror, a magnified, erect and virtual image is obtained. (ii) In case of a rectangular glass slab, emergent rays are always parallel to the direction of incident rays. Whereas when incident light passes through a prism, it bends towards the base, so incident ray and emergent ray are not parallel. (iii) f = −50 cm = −0.5 m; P = 1/f = −2 D.

subject: science
question: (i) Rohit claims to have obtained an image twice the size of object with a concave lens. Is he correct? Give reason for your answer. (ii) Where should an object be placed in case of a convex lens to form an image of same size as of the object? Show with the help of ray diagram the position and the nature of the image formed. (iii) With the help of ray diagram, illustrate the change in position, nature and size of the image formed if the convex lens in case of (ii) is replaced by concave lens of same focal length.
answer: (i) No, magnified image of an object cannot be formed by a concave lens ever. (ii) Object should be placed at 2f. Image is real, inverted and of same size. (iii) With concave lens, image is virtual, erect and diminished.

subject: science
question: In the preparation of soap, sodium chloride causes: (a) complete saponification. (b) complete hydrolysis. (c) complete neutralization. (d) complete precipitation.
answer: (a) complete saponification

subject: science
question: Vishakha carried out saponification reaction in the lab and she gave following comments as: (i) Soap is salt of fatty acid. (ii) Heat is absorbed in this reaction. (iii) Reaction mixture is basic in nature. (iv) These are neutralization reactions. The correct statements are: (a) (i) & (iii) (b) (ii) & (iv) (c) (i) & (ii) (d) (i), (ii) & (iii)
answer: (a) (i) & (iii)

subject: science
question: Select from the following salts which may be used to prepare hard water required for the experiment. A. CaSO₄  B. Na₂SO₄  C. CaCl₂  D. K₂SO₄  E. NaHCO₃  F. MgCl₂. 1. A, B and D  2. A, C and F  3. B, D and E  4. C, E and F
answer: 2. A, C and F

subject: science
question: Four students A, B, C and D did their experiment of finding the focal length of convex lens by obtaining image of a distant object as follows: Student A used the window grill in the laboratory as the object and a white paper sheet held in hand as the screen. Student B used a distant tree in the shade and a white thick board held in a stand as the screen. Student C used a well illuminated laboratory window grill as the object and a white paper sheet held in a stand as the screen. Student D used a well illuminated distant tree as the object and a white thick board held in a stand as the screen. Which student has used the correct method for performing the experiment? (a) A (b) B (c) C (d) D
answer: (d) D

subject: science
question: In the setup shown below, a clear image of a distant object is obtained on the screen. The focal length of the concave mirror is: (a) 11.4 cm (b) 9.4 cm (c) 9.8 cm (d) 9.9 cm

subject: science
question: In the experiment on refraction of light through a rectangular glass slab, the following observations were made: (A) The emergent ray moves towards the normal after second refraction with ∠i = ∠e. (B) The emergent ray moves away from the normal after second refraction ∠i ≠ ∠e. (C) For any angle of incidence, always ∠i > ∠e. (D) The emergent ray moves away from normal after second refraction with ∠i = ∠e. The student who has made the correct observation is: (a) (A) (b) (B) (c) (C) (d) (D)

subject: science
question: To trace the path of ray of light through the triangular glass prism, a student observes that the emergent ray has (a) bent away from the base of the prism (b) bent towards the base of the prism (c) moved parallel to the direction of incident ray (d) gone perpendicular to incident ray.
options: bent away from the base of the prism
bent towards the base of the prism
moved parallel to the direction of incident ray
answer: (b) bent towards the base of the prism

subject: science
question: The diagrams A and B represents the following relationship: (a) homologous organs (b) analogous organs (c) vestigial organs (d) rudimentary organs
answer: (b) analogous organs

subject: science
question: Identify the parts labeled 1 and 2 in the adjoining figure. (a) 1- micropyle, 2- epicotyls (b) 1- cotyledon, 2- embryo (c) 1-radicle, 2-plumule (d) 1-plumule, 2-radicle
answer: (d) 1-plumule, 2-radicle

subject: science
question: When you add sodium hydrogen carbonate to acetic acid in a test tube, a gas liberates immediately with a brisk effervescence. Name the gas and describe the method of its testing in the laboratory.
answer: Carbon dioxide (CO₂). Testing: Lime water turns milky when CO₂ gas is passed through it / the gas liberated extinguishes a burning splinter.

subject: science
question: Mention about the four events that occur during binary fission in amoeba.
answer: Turnip, Carrot, Sweet Potato are modified roots; Potato is a modified stem. (Note: MS answer for Q35 mentions binary fission events — four events are: (1) Nucleus elongates and divides (2) Cytoplasm divides (3) Cell membrane constricts (4) Two daughter cells formed.)

subject: science
question: A student obtained a sharp image of a lighted candle on a screen using a convex lens. Now he wants to focus a distant lamp on a far away electric pole. In which direction should the lens be moved for this purpose with respect to the screen, to get a sharp image on the screen? Justify your answer.
answer: He should move the lens towards the screen. As the distance of the object increases, the image formed by a convex lens will be closer to the focus.
